perturbation.original_text: The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Consulting Fee") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable on the last day of each month thereafter up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Consulting Fee") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable within 90 days of invoice submission, subject to the Company's financial performance, up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 3 introduces an element of uncertainty regarding the timing and certainty of payments. The original text specifies a fixed payment schedule ('payable on the last day of each month'), which is clear and binding. The changed text introduces two conditions: 'payable within 90 days of invoice submission' and 'subject to the Company's financial performance.' This directly contradicts the fixed payment schedule, creating ambiguity. Specifically, the original text provides a guaranteed payment timeline, which is now conditional on the company's financial status and payment submission. This contradicts the initial fixed payment timeline and introduces uncertainty in the enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Consulting Fee") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable within 90 days of invoice submission, subject to the Company's financial performance, up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces conditions ('payable within 90 days of invoice submission' and 'subject to the Company's financial performance') to the payment of the Consulting Fee. This directly contradicts the original fixed payment schedule ('payable on the last day of each month'), as highlighted in the explanation. The 'Contradicted Text' is identical to the 'Changed Text', meaning this new condition is now contained within the original clause. This fulfills the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition as the inconsistent terms make enforcement difficult. This creates a strong, obvious contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Consultant shall provide management and financial consulting services, including the supervision of the senior management, all staff, and all field personnel of the Company, whether employees or consultants, strategic planning and property acquisitions, strategic financial planning and annual budget reviews, as well as the implementation and monitoring of the Company's compliance with continuous reporting requirements, internal controls over accounting systems and financial reporting to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Consultant shall provide management and financial consulting services, including the supervision of the senior management, all staff, and all field personnel of the Company, whether employees or consultants, strategic planning and property acquisitions, strategic financial planning and annual budget reviews, but is not responsible for the implementation and monitoring of the Company's compliance with continuous reporting requirements, internal controls over accounting systems and financial reporting to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This modification in Schedule A creates an ambiguity regarding the Consultant's responsibility for compliance. The original text clearly states that the Consultant is responsible for 'the implementation and monitoring of the Company's compliance with continuous reporting requirements, internal controls over accounting systems and financial reporting to the Company.' The changed text explicitly removes this responsibility, creating a direct contradiction. Now, it's unclear who is ultimately responsible for ensuring compliance with these critical requirements. This can lead to disputes and enforcement difficulties. The contradiction stems from stating the consultant is responsible for the area and later not being responsible, causing a contradiction within the same section in Schedule A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THIS IS SCHEDULE "A" TO THE CONSULTING AGREEMENT BETWEEN CORAL GOLD RESOURCES LTD. AND lNTERMARK CAPlTAL CORP. Description of Consulting Services: The Consultant shall provide management and financial consulting services, including the supervision of the senior management, all staff, and all field personnel of the Company, whether employees or consultants, strategic planning and property acquisitions, strategic financial planning and annual budget reviews, but is not responsible for the implementation and monitoring of the Company's compliance with continuous reporting requirements, internal controls over accounting systems and financial reporting to the Company. Duties and Responsibilities: The Executive shall serve the Company as an executive officer in the position of President and Chief Executive Officer. The Executive shall report to the Board and shall undertake and perform the following duties and responsibilities: · actively engage with the Board to ensure that the initiatives of the management team are aligned with the strategic direction and objectives for the Company that have been established by the Board; · provide overall direction for the Company in order for it to implement agreed strategies in order to meet Company goals and objectives; · provide shareholder and investor communication and manage key investment banking and institutional relationships; · make decisions in line with organizational goals, leading to desired results, and will be responsible and accountable for results; · create and sustain the organizational culture and environment needed to achieve objectives and results and recruit and retain a high-performance operating team; · oversee the implementation and monitoring of internal controls, reporting compliance obligations, sign off on CEO Certificates for the interim and annual financial statements and setting environmental protection policies; and · such other duties and responsibilities as may be assigned or vested in him by the Board from time to time and which are consistent with the duties and responsibilities of a President and Chief Executive Officer. The Executive agrees, during the continuance of his employment, to devote sufficient working time, services, skill and ability to such employment and to serve at all times with loyalty and honesty in the best interests of the Company. The Executive acknowledges that the position of President and CEO will involve significant travel for business development and for investor relations.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Schedule A assigns the Consultant responsibility for 'the implementation and monitoring of the Company's compliance with continuous reporting requirements, internal controls over accounting systems and financial reporting to the Company.' The changed text explicitly removes this responsibility from the Consultant. The contradicted text in Schedule A, under 'Duties and Responsibilities', states that the Executive shall 'oversee the implementation and monitoring of internal controls, reporting compliance obligations, sign off on CEO Certificates for the interim and annual financial statements and setting environmental protection policies.' By removing the Consultant's responsibility for compliance, it creates an ambiguity and potential conflict as to who is ultimately responsible for these critical compliance functions, directly contradicting the Executive's responsibilities as outlined in the same schedule A of the agreement. This is a clear and direct contradiction, aligning with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the parties, the Consultant may not act as an agent of the Company; however, this does not and is not intended to restrict the powers of the Principal to act as President and Chief Executive Officer of the Company in any way. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Consultant shall not commit or be entitled to commit the Company to any obligation whatsoever nor shall the Consultant incur or be entitled to incur any debt or liability whatsoever on behalf of the Company, except as otherwise agreed to by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the parties, the Consultant may act as an agent of the Company, but only with the written consent from the board of directors regarding a specific action; however, this does not and is not intended to restrict the powers of the Principal to act as President and Chief Executive Officer of the Company in any way. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Consultant shall not commit or be entitled to commit the Company to any obligation whatsoever nor shall the Consultant incur or be entitled to incur any debt or liability whatsoever on behalf of the Company, except as otherwise agreed to by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 14 changes the Consultant's agency status. The original text states the Consultant 'may not act as an agent,' providing a clear restriction. The changed text now states the Consultant 'may act as an agent of the Company, but only with the written consent from the board of directors regarding a specific action'. This introduces a conditional agency, contradicting the initial prohibition. While this section allows the consultant to act as an agent of the Company with written consent, the sentence 'Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Consultant shall not commit or be entitled to commit the Company to any obligation whatsoever nor shall the Consultant incur or be entitled to incur any debt or liability whatsoever on behalf of the Company, except as otherwise agreed to by the Company', creates a contradiction as it limits the Consultants ability to commit to anything, even with written consent, which is contradicting. The question then arises regarding, even if written consent is there, the consultant wouldn't be able to do anything. This can lead to disputes and enforcement difficulties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the parties, the Consultant may act as an agent of the Company, but only with the written consent from the board of directors regarding a specific action; however, this does not and is not intended to restrict the powers of the Principal to act as President and Chief Executive Officer of the Company in any way. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Consultant shall not commit or be entitled to commit the Company to any obligation whatsoever nor shall the Consultant incur or be entitled to incur any debt or liability whatsoever on behalf of the Company, except as otherwise agreed to by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Consultant to act as an agent with written consent, but simultaneously restricts their ability to commit the Company to any obligation, even with that consent. This creates a direct conflict because an agent typically has the power to bind their principal, but the changed text negates this power. The contradiction arises because the text states: 'the Consultant may act as an agent of the Company, but only with the written consent from the board of directors regarding a specific action' but then continues with 'Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Consultant shall not commit or be entitled to commit the Company to any obligation whatsoever nor shall the Consultant incur or be entitled to incur any debt or liability whatsoever on behalf of the Company, except as otherwise agreed to by the Company.' This makes the scope of the agency ambiguous and unenforceable, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.
